content.problemStatement:
The score of a permutation PPP of integers [1,N][1, N][1,N] is defined as follows:

∑L=1N∑R=LNMEX(P[L,R])\sum_{L = 1}^{N} \sum_{R = L}^{N} \text{MEX}(P[L, R])∑L=1N​∑R=LN​MEX(P[L,R])

where P[L,R]P[L, R]P[L,R] denotes the subarray [PL,PL+1,PL+2,…,PR][P_L, P_{L + 1}, P_{L + 2}, \ldots, P_R][PL​,PL+1​,PL+2​,…,PR​] and MEX(A)\text{MEX}(A)MEX(A) represents the minimal non-negative integer not present in AAA.

You are given a partially filled permutation QQQ, i.e. QQQ is formed by replacing some (possibly, none or all) of the elements of a permutation PPP with −1-1−1.

Your task is to replace the −1-1−1s appropriately to form a permutation and maximize the score of the permutation. Print the maximum possible score.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN - the size of the permutation PPP.
The second line contains NNN integers - Q1,Q2,…,QNQ_1, Q_2, \ldots, Q_NQ1​,Q2​,…,QN​, the partially filled in permutation PPP.




Output Format
For each test case, print the maximum score possible when appropriately replacing the −1-1−1s in QQQ to form a permutation.
constraints: All the non −1-1−1 elements of QQQ are distinct
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500050005000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are no −1-1−1s, so P=[0,1,2]P = [0, 1, 2]P=[0,1,2]. We can calculate the score as the sum of MEX-es over all subarrays, which will be 666.
Test Case 2 : We can choose P=[1,0,2]P = [1, 0, 2]P=[1,0,2], and this obtains the maximum possible score of 777.
